user_query:
I want my laptop to automatically switch on Do Not Disturb when I go live and log a short promo message for my matcha tea bar. Please wire this up locally using the files in this workspace and produce verifiable outputs.

Requirements:
1) Use input/calendar.ics and extract only events whose SUMMARY contains "Live Stream" (case-insensitive). Treat the timestamps as UTC (they end with Z). Use input/matcha_menu.html to extract the first 3 drinks from the menu (each li.menu-item has span.name and span.price).

2) Create scripts/build_schedule.py and run it. It must:
   - Parse the ICS and HTML as above.
   - Build a single promo message for each live event in the format: "Going live now! Featured drinks: <Name1> (<Price1>), <Name2> (<Price2>), <Name3> (<Price3>)."
   - Write output/promos.json containing an array of objects, one per selected event, with fields: uid (string), start_utc (RFC3339 timestamp from DTSTART), end_utc (RFC3339 timestamp from DTEND), message (string).
   - Generate config/cron/matcha_focus.cron with the first non-comment line being exactly "CRON_TZ=UTC". For each event, append two cron entries using UTC minute/hour/day/month fields:
     - At the event start: "<min> <hour> <dom> <mon> * bash scripts/toggle_focus.sh on \"<message>\""
     - At the event end:   "<min> <hour> <dom> <mon> * bash scripts/toggle_focus.sh off"
     Include a comment line immediately above each pair in the form "# UID: <uid> <start_utc> -> <end_utc>".

3) Modify scripts/toggle_focus.sh so it actually does something locally:
   - Ensure output/logs exists.
   - When called with "on" or "off", append a line to output/logs/focus.log with an ISO timestamp and either "DND ON" or "DND OFF".
   - When called with "on" and a non-empty message argument, read config/notifications.yaml and use the notification_command under the profile named matcha_promo to write the message into output/logs/notifications.log (the command should receive the message with {message} substituted).

4) Update config/notifications.yaml by adding a profile named matcha_promo with a notification_command that appends the given message to output/logs/notifications.log (for example, via an echo template with {message}). Leave the existing default profile intact.

5) After you generate the schedule, run: python scripts/build_schedule.py. Then simulate the first event’s start by running scripts/toggle_focus.sh with action "on" and that event’s message so both output/logs/focus.log and output/logs/notifications.log get a new entry.

Deliverables and verification paths:
- output/promos.json: Must include exactly the live stream events from input/calendar.ics and messages built from the first 3 items in input/matcha_menu.html.
- config/cron/matcha_focus.cron: Must begin with CRON_TZ=UTC and contain two cron lines per live event with correct UTC fields and the command lines as specified.
- scripts/toggle_focus.sh: Implemented behavior per above (logs and notification execution).
- config/notifications.yaml: Contains the new matcha_promo profile.
- output/logs/focus.log and output/logs/notifications.log: Must contain at least one new entry from your simulation run.

Use only the provided files as inputs; do not download anything. Place all outputs exactly at the paths specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
